_comment_1: Advancement Guide - english. The 'guia' keys are the HOW TO GET IT text
_comment_2: for each advancement. They can be changed with a resource pack, or per player
_comment_3: in config/advancementguide-guias.json, which takes priority over these.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.adventur: Kill five different mobs with a single crossbow shot using the Multishot enchantment. Group several enemies together, wait until they line up and fire when all of them are within reach of the three arrows.
Avoid setting off a Sculk Sensor or a Warden by walking on wool blocks, or by placing wool between you and the sensor. Wool blocks vibrations, letting you move without being detected.
Kill a Breeze using its own projectile. Make the wind charge bounce off a block or interact with the surroundings until it hits the Breeze back. It is easiest inside a Trial Chamber.
Find an armadillo in warm biomes such as savannas or badlands and use a brush on it to get an Armadillo Scute. You do not need to hurt it.
Hit the exact centre of a target block from at least 30 blocks away using a bow or a crossbow. Building a straight platform makes measuring the distance easier.
Craft a decorated pot using only four pottery sherds found at archaeology sites. Do not use plain bricks in the recipe.
Build a setup using an automatic Crafter to craft another Crafter. Feed the ingredients in with redstone and let the block complete the recipe on its own.

Fall from the highest point of the world down to the deepest one and survive. The easiest way is to jump with an Elytra and land in water, on a honey block, in powder snow, or by using a water bucket right before impact.

Get a Creaking Heart from a tree in the Pale Garden biome and place it correctly between two Pale Oak logs to create a new Creaking. The advancement is granted once the heart is transplanted successfully.
Win a full raid, started by entering a village with the Bad Omen effect. When it ends you get the Hero of the Village effect, which gives you big discounts when trading with villagers.
Slide down the side of a honey block for several blocks to slow your fall. You can build a tall tower with a honey block on one side and drop down along it.
Kill any hostile mob, such as a zombie, skeleton, creeper or spider. It is one of the first advancements you will get during your first night.
Place a Sculk Catalyst and kill a mob near it. When the enemy dies, the catalyst turns nearby blocks into Sculk and the advancement is granted.
Use a glow ink sac on a sign to make its text glow in the dark. Glow ink sacs come from killing Glow Squids in water caves.

Place a lightning rod near a village before a thunderstorm. When lightning strikes the rod instead of a villager or a building, you get the advancement. Putting the rod on the highest point of the village works best.
Find a Trial Chamber by exploring underground and set off a Trial Spawner for the first time by getting close to it. The challenge starts automatically.
Craft a crossbow using 3 sticks, 2 strings, an iron ingot and a tripwire hook. You can also get one by killing pillagers.
Deal 50 points of damage or more in a single hit. The easiest way is to use a fully charged mace after falling from a great height, taking advantage of its extra impact damage.
Find a Meadow biome, place a jukebox and put any music disc in it. The advancement unlocks as soon as it starts playing.
Open a Vault inside a Trial Chamber using a Trial Key earned by completing the challenges there. Each player needs their own key to get rewards.
This one is granted automatically the first time you enter a world in survival mode, and it is the start of the Adventure branch.
Fire an arrow using a working bow. Just draw the bow and hit anything, whether a mob or a block.
Sleep in a bed at night or during a thunderstorm. Besides the advancement, this sets your respawn point to that bed.
Kill a skeleton from at least 50 blocks away using a bow or a crossbow. Building a straight platform to measure the distance makes it much easier.
Kill several mobs with a single trident throw using the right Impaling enchantment and lining the enemies up. Herding them into a narrow corridor improves your chances.
Look at the Ender Dragon through a spyglass while it is flying. Just keep it in view for a moment during the fight.
Use a spyglass to look at a Ghast in the Nether. Best done from a sheltered spot so you do not take fireballs while you do it.
Find a parrot in a jungle biome and look at it through a spyglass. You do not need to tame it.
Build an iron golem by placing four iron blocks in a T shape with a carved pumpkin on top. The golem will automatically protect villagers from hostile mobs.
Throw a trident by holding down the attack button until the throw is fully charged. Tridents drop from Drowned, though not always.
Hold a Totem of Undying in your main or off hand and take a killing blow. The totem is used up, saves you from dying and gives you regeneration, absorption and fire resistance.
Trade with any villager using emeralds or whatever items they ask for. The first completed trade grants the advancement.
Trade with a villager near the world's build limit. Build a tall platform, move a villager up there with rails or boats, and trade with them once you are at the top.
Kill two mobs with a single arrow. The easiest way is to line up two low-health enemies in a narrow corridor and shoot a crossbow with the Piercing enchantment, or use already weakened enemies.
Open a Vault using an Ominous Trial Key, earned by clearing a Trial Chamber on ominous difficulty. These vaults give better rewards.
Place a Lodestone and right-click it with a compass. The compass is permanently linked to that block and will point to it even in dimensions where it normally would not work.
During a thunderstorm, use a trident with Channeling to call lightning down on a mob. The enchantment only works if the target is out in the open and it is raining.
Kill a Pillager Captain carrying a banner on its head to get the Bad Omen effect. That effect starts a raid when you enter a village.
Put on leather boots and walk over powder snow. Without the boots you sink in and start freezing.
Stay in the air for several seconds using only an Elytra and the wind bursts made by a Breeze, with no rockets. Look for a Trial Chamber with enough room to glide in.
Kill a Pillager using a crossbow. You can craft your own or pick up one dropped by another pillager. Keep your distance and use cover so you do not take too many shots.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.root: Enter the End for the first time. You need to activate a stronghold portal with Eyes of Ender and jump in. The game's own description is a joke, so here is what matters: bring good armour, food, a bow with plenty of arrows and blocks, because you cannot leave without dying or beating the Dragon.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.kill: Beat the Ender Dragon by destroying every End Crystal first so it cannot heal. Use a bow for the ones on top of the tall pillars, and hit it with a sword or axe while it perches on the portal.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.drag: After beating the Dragon, the Dragon Egg appears on top of the central portal. Do not mine it directly or it will teleport away. The easiest way is to place a torch two blocks below it, break the block holding it up, and let it fall onto the torch so you can pick it up.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.resp: Place four End Crystals around the central End portal, one on each side. Placing the fourth one starts the summoning and the Dragon comes back.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.ente: After beating the Dragon, an End Gateway appears floating near the main island. Throw an Ender Pearl into the small portal to travel to the outer End islands.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.find: Explore the outer End islands until you find an End City. These structures hold Shulkers, chests with great loot and sometimes an End Ship with an Elytra.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.elyt: Find an End Ship next to an End City and take the Elytra hanging inside it. Bring plenty of Ender Pearls and blocks to make exploring the islands easier.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.levi: Get the Levitation effect for at least 50 blocks of height. Let a Shulker hit you with one of its projectiles and avoid touching walls or the ground until you have risen far enough. A water bucket or an Elytra can save you when the effect wears off.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.end.drag: During the Ender Dragon fight, wait until it leaves a purple breath cloud on the ground and use a Glass Bottle on it to collect Dragon's Breath. You need this item to make lingering potions.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.husbandr: This one is granted automatically when you start the Husbandry branch. It is all about farming, breeding, fishing and looking after animals.
Breed two animals of the same species using their favourite food. For example: wheat for cows and sheep, carrots for pigs, seeds for chickens or golden carrots for horses.
Tame any mob that can be tamed. The easiest is to use bones on a wolf or raw fish on a cat until hearts appear.
Find an axolotl in a water cave below sea level and scoop it up with a water bucket. You get a Bucket of Axolotl.
Get a Froglight by making a frog eat a small magma cube. Depending on the frog variant you get a different Froglight: pearlescent, ochre or verdant.
Take an axolotl with you while you fight an aquatic mob. Once the axolotl joins in and the enemy dies, you get a short regeneration effect and your mining fatigue is removed.
Write any text on a sign and use a Glow Ink Sac on it to make the letters glow, so you can read them at night.
Upgrade a diamond hoe to netherite using a Netherite Upgrade Smithing Template, a netherite ingot and a Smithing Table. It is not a tool you use much, which is exactly the joke.
Get a Sniffer Egg, either through archaeology at warm ocean ruins or by breeding two adult Sniffers. Place it on moss blocks to hatch it faster.

Feed a Snifflet, the Sniffer's baby, with Torchflower Seeds. To get one: find a Sniffer Egg (through archaeology at warm ocean ruins), hatch it on moss, and feed that Snifflet. The seeds are dug up by adult Sniffers themselves.
Get a Dried Ghast and place it in water. After staying hydrated for a while it returns to normal and the advancement is granted.
Put Wolf Armor, crafted from Armadillo Scutes, on a tamed wolf, then take it off again by interacting with the wolf with shears.
When a wolf's armour has taken damage, use Armadillo Scutes on the wolf to repair it fully. You do not need to craft a new set.
Get a goat into a boat by pushing or luring it in, then ride the same boat with it. Goats spawn in mountain biomes.
When a nest or hive is at max honey (dripping honey), place a campfire right underneath and use glass bottles to collect the honey. The smoke stops the bees from getting angry.
Use a Silk Touch tool to collect a bee nest with at least three bees inside. Placing a campfire below stops them attacking you.
Scoop up a tadpole with a water bucket. Tadpoles appear when two frogs breed and the eggs hatch.
Remove the wax from any waxed copper block using an axe. This also brings back copper's normal oxidising behaviour.
Use a honeycomb on any copper block to wax it and stop it oxidising over time.
Give any item to an Allay and wait for it to bring it back. If the Allay finds more of the same item lying around, it picks those up too before returning to you.
Give a cake to an Allay and play a nearby note block so the Allay uses it as its drop-off point. When it leaves the cake by the note block you get the advancement.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.r: Enter the Nether for the first time through an obsidian portal. Bring armour, food, blocks and a shield, because it is a very dangerous dimension.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.f: Explore the Nether until you find a Nether Fortress. They usually show up over lava oceans or wide open spaces. Blazes and Wither Skeletons live there.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.o: Kill a Blaze inside a Nether Fortress. Use a shield to block its fireballs and get a Blaze Rod, which you need for Blaze Powder and to reach the End.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.b: Craft a Brewing Stand with a Blaze Rod and cobblestone. Add a Water Bottle, Nether Wart and any ingredient to brew your first potion.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.a: Get the effect of every available potion at least once. You will need to brew strength, speed, regeneration, night vision, fire resistance, water breathing, invisibility, slowness, harming, poison, weakness, slow falling and the other variants.

Have every status effect at once. This takes serious planning: beacon, dolphin, village, raid, potions, enchanted golden apple, shulker, elder guardian, Wither, the Warden's darkness and many more. Build a dedicated setup and apply the effects in a planned order so they do not run out.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.f: Find a Bastion Remnant by exploring the Nether. These huge structures hold Piglins, chests with good loot and netherite upgrade template pieces.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.o: Get Crying Obsidian by bartering with Piglins, or find it in chests at Bastions and Ruined Portals.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.c: Craft a Respawn Anchor from Crying Obsidian and Glowstone. Charge it by right-clicking with Glowstone blocks so you can respawn in the Nether.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.r: Put a saddle on a Strider and ride it. To steer it use a Warped Fungus on a Stick, just like a carrot on a stick for pigs.
Take a Strider to the Overworld through a portal and ride it on a lava lake. Getting it there is the hard part, so build a safe path to the portal first.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.f: Travel 7 kilometres in the Overworld by using the Nether. Since one Nether block equals eight Overworld blocks, you only need to walk about 875 blocks in the Nether before coming out of another portal.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.o: Mine Ancient Debris with a diamond or netherite pickaxe. It is mostly found between Y 12 and Y 18. Beds or TNT are a very efficient way to find it.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.n: Upgrade all four diamond armour pieces using Netherite Ingots and an Upgrade Template found in a Bastion. Then wear the full set.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.g: Get a Wither Skeleton Skull by killing wither skeletons in a Nether Fortress. Looting III raises the drop chance a lot.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.s: Place four Soul Sand or Soul Soil blocks in a T shape and put three Wither Skulls on top. The Wither appears after a few seconds of charging.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.c: Beat the Wither to get a Nether Star. Craft a Beacon and place it on a base of iron, gold, diamond, emerald or netherite to activate it.
Build a complete four-level pyramid (164 mineral blocks in total) under a Beacon. That unlocks all of its powers and completes the advancement.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.r: Hit a fireball thrown by a Ghast to send it back and kill it with its own projectile. You can use a sword or any melee swing to deflect it.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.nether.u: Take a Ghast from the Nether to the Overworld and kill it there. The easiest way is to find one near the portal, lure it through wide tunnels or with shots, and get it across the portal before killing it. Bring plenty of blocks to protect the portal from the explosions.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.ro: The game's first advancement. Granted automatically the first time you enter a survival world.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.mi: Break stone blocks with a wooden pickaxe to get cobblestone. Then craft a stone pickaxe to keep progressing.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.up: Use the cobblestone you got to craft any stone tool at a crafting table. The stone pickaxe is usually the best pick to move forward.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.sm: Mine iron ore and smelt it in a furnace using coal, charcoal or any other fuel. Get at least one iron ingot.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.ir: Craft an iron pickaxe with 3 ingots and 2 sticks. This pickaxe lets you mine diamond, gold, redstone and other advanced ores.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.mi: Find diamond ore in the deep layers of the world; Y -59 is one of the best heights. Use an iron pickaxe or better to mine it. With Fortune III you get more diamonds.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.la: Fill an iron bucket with lava by right-clicking a lava source block. Be careful not to get too close so you do not burn.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.fo: Get an obsidian block by pouring water over source lava, or by mining it with a diamond or netherite pickaxe. You need obsidian to build a Nether portal.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.en: Build a Nether portal with at least 10 obsidian blocks and light it with flint and steel, or anything else that makes fire. Step through the portal to get the advancement.
Enchant any item at an enchanting table. You need experience, lapis lazuli and the table itself. Placing up to 15 bookshelves around it unlocks the highest level enchantments.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.cu: Weaken a zombie villager with a Potion of Weakness (splash or via arrow) and then give it a Golden Apple. Wait 2 to 5 minutes while it cures. Keeping it locked up stops it dying during the process.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.fo: Craft an Eye of Ender by combining an Ender Pearl with Blaze Powder. Throw it in the air and follow it to find the stronghold with the End portal.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.en: Find the stronghold, activate the portal by placing 12 Eyes of Ender (or however many are missing) and enter the End. Bring good armour, food, a bow and blocks before you jump in.
advancementguide.guia.minecraft.story.de: Make an arrow shot by a skeleton bounce off a shield. Raise the shield just before impact to block the projectile properly.
